axis: bridge / time-shape alert rules — consecutive_warn streak + recent_warn_ratio window
title: atlas_alert_subscriber.hexa — add streak-threshold + window-minutes rule modes
summary: Extend tool/atlas_alert_subscriber.hexa (ω-bridge-17) with two time-shape alert rules that the original ratio-only rules cannot detect. (a) consecutive_warn — fires when the last K matching rows are ALL WARN, catching hot anomalies that started recently and which a moderate ratio would dilute. (b) recent_warn_ratio — fires when the WARN-ratio over the last M minutes (parsed from the ts field) >= --warn-threshold, escalating closer-in-time anomalies faster than a fixed-N tail. Both are off-by-default (K=0 / M=0); existing ratio rules and SKIP/PASS semantics unchanged when new flags are absent.

predecessors.omega_bridge_16: promoted __BRIDGE_ANOMALY__ sentinel into a structured sibling JSONL row scoped 'bridge_anomaly' with ISO-8601 ts field
predecessors.omega_bridge_17: built tool/atlas_alert_subscriber.hexa — JSONL-stream subscriber with warn_ratio + insufficient_ratio rules
predecessors.omega_bridge_18: wired -L flag into bridge_health.sh so the subscriber runs as part of the single-CLI sweep

context.problem: Ratio-only rules over a fixed-N tail miss two important time-shape patterns. A bridge that starts failing 5 minutes ago shows up as a 4/10 = 0.4 ratio (right at or below the default warn-threshold) even though every recent row is WARN — the older PASS rows dilute the signal. Likewise, 'last 24h with 3 WARNs in the most recent hour' is operationally far more urgent than '24h with 3 WARNs spread evenly', but warn_ratio sees them identically.

design_alternatives_considered.option: A — exponential decay weighting on ratio compute
design_alternatives_considered.rejected_because: loses interpretability ('why did it fire?') and requires a half-life knob that operators have no calibration data for yet

design_alternatives_considered.chosen_because: two orthogonal levers, each with a single intuitive parameter; can be enabled independently; sentinels carry observed/streak/ratio for forensic readback; both off-by-default preserves existing-behaviour

design_alternatives_considered.rejected_because: wasteful surface; the read-pipeline (load → filter scope → tally) is identical and would be duplicated; raw 9 hexa-only-suffix is satisfied either way but maintenance is higher

edits: header sentinels list: added FIRED rule=consecutive_warn and FIRED rule=recent_warn_ratio shapes
header rules section: documented --streak-threshold K and --window-minutes M (both default 0 = disabled)
argv parser: --streak-threshold and --window-minutes flag handlers; --help text extended

consecutive_warn rule body: walks tail_lines from end, counts trailing consecutive WARN rows, fires when observed >= K; rules_evaluated incremented; emits __ATLAS_ALERT__ FIRED rule=consecutive_warn streak=K observed=K last_n=N sampled=S

recent_warn_ratio rule body: writes joined matched-rows to /tmp/atlas_alert_window_<ts>_<n>.jsonl, shells out to python3 to parse ISO-8601 ts and compute WARN-ratio over [now-M*60, now] (skipping future-dated and parse-failed rows defensively), reuses --warn-threshold as the firing threshold; emits __ATLAS_ALERT__ FIRED rule=recent_warn_ratio ratio=R threshold=T window_minutes=M sampled=S; tmp file cleaned up via rm -f

new_sentinels: __ATLAS_ALERT__ FIRED rule=consecutive_warn streak=K observed=K last_n=N sampled=S
__ATLAS_ALERT__ FIRED rule=recent_warn_ratio ratio=R threshold=T window_minutes=M sampled=S

edge_cases_handled.streak_non_matching_skipped: tail_lines is already filtered to scope=bridge_anomaly upstream, so the streak walk operates only on matching rows by construction (the spec's 'skip non-matching to compute streak only on matching rows' edge case is satisfied automatically)
edge_cases_handled.window_parse_failure: python3 try/except around datetime.strptime — any row whose ts cannot be parsed is silently skipped (raw 71 report-only)
edge_cases_handled.window_future_dated: explicit `if t > now: continue` excludes future-dated rows from the count (defensive — prevents a clock-skewed producer from inflating recent_warn_ratio)
edge_cases_handled.window_no_in_window_rows: if win_total == 0 the rule is treated as 'nothing to evaluate' and does NOT fire (avoids divide-by-zero and avoids false positives over an empty sample)
edge_cases_handled.both_rules_simultaneous: consecutive_warn and recent_warn_ratio fire independently of each other and of the existing warn_ratio / insufficient_ratio rules; each emits its own __ATLAS_ALERT__ line
edge_cases_handled.compat_when_flags_absent: DEFAULT_STREAK="0" and DEFAULT_WINDOW_MIN="0" gate the new blocks behind != "0" checks; rules_evaluated stays at 2 (matching pre-bridge-19 baseline)

compliance.raw_9_hexa_only_suffix: edited an existing tracked .hexa file; no new non-.hexa files created
compliance.raw_71_report_only: tool remains read-only on the timeline; tmp file is in /tmp/ and cleaned up; rule firing does not change exit code
compliance.raw_73_admissibility: scope=bridge_anomaly filter unchanged; new rules operate on the same matched rowset
compliance.raw_77_append_only_respect: tool does not append to atlas_health_timeline.jsonl
compliance.raw_80_sentinel: two new __ATLAS_ALERT__ FIRED shapes added; existing PASS / SKIP / FIRED shapes untouched
compliance.no_parallel_session_collision: edited only tool/atlas_alert_subscriber.hexa; did not touch cli/run.hexa, state/atlas_health_timeline.jsonl, bisociation/spectra/g_atlas_composite_v3.json, tool/omega_cycle_atlas_ingest.hexa, n6/*, tool/kick_*.hexa, design/kick/*, .own

fixtures.K=5_observed: only __ATLAS_ALERT__ FIRED rule=warn_ratio (existing rule); no consecutive_warn line

fixtures.name: F2 streak_broken — last 4 are WARN+PASS+WARN+WARN
fixtures.K=3_expected: consecutive_warn does NOT fire (broken streak — observed=2)

fixtures.M=60_thr=0.5_observed: __ATLAS_ALERT__ FIRED rule=recent_warn_ratio ratio=0.6000 threshold=0.5 window_minutes=60 sampled=5

fixtures.name: F4 compat — no new flags
fixtures.observed: __ATLAS_ALERT__ PASS sampled=10 rules_evaluated=2; human summary shows PASS=7 WARN=3 INSUFFICIENT_DATA=0; ratios warn=0.3000 / insuf=0.0000; 'no rules fired'

fixtures.name: edge — future-dated + broken-ts rows
fixtures.fixture: /tmp/ob19/window_edge.jsonl (1 future-dated WARN, 1 broken-ts WARN, 1 in-window WARN, 1 too-old PASS)
fixtures.M=60_thr=0.5_expected: future + broken-ts skipped, only the in-window WARN counted, ratio=1.0
fixtures.M=60_thr=0.5_observed: __ATLAS_ALERT__ FIRED rule=recent_warn_ratio ratio=1.0000 threshold=0.5 window_minutes=60 sampled=1

fixtures.name: all-rules-together
fixtures.observed: warn_ratio + consecutive_warn + recent_warn_ratio all fire on the same sweep, three independent __ATLAS_ALERT__ lines emitted

operational_notes.wiring_into_bridge_health_sh: ω-bridge-19 does NOT modify bridge_health.sh; operators who want streak/window rules in the cron pipeline can extend ALERT_*_THR env vars in a future cycle. The subscriber accepts the new flags directly when invoked outside the -L wrapper.
calibration_guidance: --streak-threshold 3 catches a fresh outage within ~3 sweeps (~3-15 minutes typical cron cadence)
--window-minutes 60 with --warn-threshold 0.5 catches 'majority WARN over the last hour' regardless of how long the timeline is
for high-cadence sweeps, reduce --window-minutes (e.g. 15) and tighten threshold; for low-cadence, increase both
operational_notes.back_compat: all existing invocations continue to work; both new flags default to 0 (disabled)

future_candidates: ALERT_STREAK_K and ALERT_WINDOW_MIN env-vars in bridge_health.sh -L wrapper (one-line addition each)
exponential-decay weighted ratio as a third time-shape rule
fingerprint suppression — don't refire the same alert within a cooldown window
